items.title: 5th grader raises $2,000 for grieving teacher
Condo owners remember building scare
10-year-old shoot mom over VR headset
Fire crews respond to fire at boarded up building
Milwaukee Dancing Grannies planning return
HS football players gain perspective helping vets
She made a promise to her husband to keep their tree farm going
Almost 200 animals rescued from puppy mill
Water study could delay commercial construction
Girl who called 911 for her father honored
Hospitals becoming a 'dumping ground' for kids
Christmas parade road closures and detours
Cars totaling nearly $200k stolen from dealership
Mother fends off raccoon from daughter
Car chase ends with police-involved shooting
$200,000 worth of supplies donated to PB schools
Family throws surprise birthday party at Eagles tailgate for Vietnam vet
President Trump vows revenge on Iran for killing U.S. service members
items.summary: After a weekend of deadly attacks, the Pentagon has released the identities of some of the fallen service members, including 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan from Hawaii and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales from Texas, who were killed from an Iranian attack. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports from the region.

items.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
items.summary: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

items.title: Democrats avoid a messy fight in Maine, but uncertainty lingers: From the Politics Desk
items.summary: Welcome to From the Politics Desk, a daily newsletter that brings you the NBC News Politics team’s latest reporting and analysis from the White House, Capitol Hill and the campaign trail.

items.title: Alaska wants to seize pilot's plane over a 6-pack. Supreme Court will hear his case
items.summary: The Supreme Court has agreed to review an Alaska bush pilot’s claims that state authorities have tried to illegally seize his $95,000 airplane after a passenger brought a six-pack of beer on a flight bound for a dry village

items.title: 10 things that wowed us at the 2026 World Cup
items.summary: The United States, Mexico, and Canada co-hosted the nearly six-week tournament. NPR's team of journalists who covered the World Cup reflects on the experience.

items.title: Attempt to save a struggling swimmer leaves 5 dead in an Ohio river
items.summary: Authorities say five people have died on a river in Ohio after several of them went into the water to assist another person who was struggling to swim

items.title: Trial begins for mom who says postpartum issues led her to kill 3 children
items.summary: Jury selection has begun in the murder trial of a Massachusetts woman who is expected to argue that postpartum issues were the reason she killed her three children

items.title: Tate brothers fight extradition to UK over rape and sex trafficking charges
items.summary: A lawyer says influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate will fight extradition to the United Kingdom

items.title: Sen. Darline Graham says she is running for a full 6-year term after Trump urged her to jump into the race
items.summary: President Donald Trump said he had given his “complete and total endorsement” to Graham, who was appointed to finish her late brother’s Senate term.

items.title: Tate brothers in Miami court after new U.K. charges
items.summary: Andrew and Tristan Tate appeared in a Miami courtroom after British prosecutors unveiled a wave of new charges, including rape. Authorities from the U.K. are seeking to extradite the Tate brothers. NBC News' Danny Cevallos reports on the 38 charges they face.

items.title: Hundreds gather for Nolan Wells’ funeral in Mississippi
items.summary: Nolan Wells, the Mississippi teenager whose death after a Fourth of July boating trip with friends has spurred national attention, was remembered during a funeral that attracted hundreds. NBC News' Aaron Gilchrist reports on what Wells' mother said during the service and the incident report distributed by police.

items.title: Court ruling freezes Paramount-Warner Bros. merger for now
items.summary: Paramount's owners were hoping they'd be firmly in control of rival Warner Bros. Discovery by the end of this week. Monday's temporary restraining order puts that plan on hold.

items.title: FDA says it’s still investigating Taylor Farms in cyclosporiasis outbreak
items.summary: Taylor Farms continues to be the subject of a Food and Drug Administration investigation into the growing cyclosporiasis outbreak, despite a false positive test result, the agency said Monday.

items.title: Judge temporarily pauses Paramount-Warner Bros. deal
items.summary: A federal judge temporarily paused the proposed merger of Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. Discovery, granting a request from a coalition of 12 state attorneys general who sued to thwart the $110 billion deal.

items.title: Judge pauses Paramount-Warner merger
items.summary: Twelve state attorneys general requested a temporary restraining order barring Paramount Skydance from closing the blockbuster deal.

items.title: Ex-Olympian charged with damaging Reflecting Pool gets Sept. 28 trial date
items.summary: A judge in the nation’s capital has set a September trial date for a former Olympic canoe racer charged with deliberately damaging the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool

items.title: India's Gen Z 'Cockroach People's Party' started as satire but is serious about change
items.summary: They call themselves the "cockroaches" and they're on the march — demanding action against corruption, amid a shortage of opportunities for young people in India.

items.title: Here’s how often you should change your towels, and when to throw them away, according to experts
items.summary: I talked with laundry experts to learn how often you should change ans wash your towels and when it’s best to throw them away in exchange for new ones.

items.title: Pamela Smart, serving life, seeks a new trial
items.summary: Lawyers for Pamela Smart, who's serving life for orchestrating her husband's 1990 murder, are hoping that a New Hampshire judge will allow her latest petition for a new trial

items.title: From The Sports Desk: Spain is the world champion
items.summary: An extra-time goal by Ferran Torres gave the Spaniards their second world title and the first since 2010.

items.title: Oystercatchers, known for sharp bills and beady eyes, are making a comeback
items.summary: A shorebird known for its sharp, orange bill and beady eyes is making a comeback in North America thanks to a yearslong conservation effort

items.title: Putin meets North Korean foreign minister in diplomatic push
items.summary: The visit by North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui comes without an obvious occasion, such as a multilateral diplomatic event or state anniversary, and has raised speculation that she may be coordinating a possible visit to Russia by North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.

items.title: Is the Oura Ring 5 worth it?
items.summary: The Oura Ring 5 is significantly smaller and slimmer than previous versions. But is it worth the upgrade? I tested it for over a month to find out.

items.title: How to keep babies safe when temperatures soar
items.summary: Infants, toddlers and young children need outdoor activity to build strong bodies and minds, but they also require extra care in the heat

items.title: Andy Burnham formally appointed U.K. prime minster
items.summary: Andy Burnham was formally appointed the newest British prime minster, the seventh to take up the position in the last decade. NBC News’ Raf Sanchez reports on how Burnham differs from outgoing prime minster Keir Starmer and his meeting with King Charles III.

items.title: 3 U.S. personnel killed in Iran conflict. And, Hunter Biden opens up to NPR
items.summary: Three U.S. service members have been killed since Friday amid rising tension between the U.S. and Iran. And, Hunter Biden talks to NPR's Newsmakers about addiction and public scrutiny.

items.title: US military identifies 2 soldiers killed in Iranian attack on Jordan
items.summary: The U.S. military has identified two soldiers killed in action last week as part of the war with Iran

items.title: We want to hear how Medicaid work requirements are affecting you
items.summary: If you have lost your health coverage or are at risk of losing it, tell us your story.

items.title: At least 930 people have died in Congo's Ebola outbreak
items.summary: Congo's health ministry said the deaths were recorded out of the 2,344 cases confirmed as of Saturday. Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest totals.

items.title: U.S. and Iran launch new strikes after 3 U.S. service members are killed
items.summary: The U.S. attacks were the ninth consecutive day of bombing since peace talks between the two countries collapsed in dispute over a memorandum of understanding focused on the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Deloitte-run systems denied Medicaid to disabled people. New laws could make it worse
items.summary: Complex computer systems that decide whether people get Medicaid benefits have a history of errors. Now, changes to comply with Trump's tax and domestic policy law are inundating those systems.

items.title: So distressed you can't think straight? Lower the intensity with these 4 skills
items.summary: A clinical psychologist shares effective strategies to quickly de-escalate stress and anxiety. That includes dunking your head into a bowl of ice water to trigger your mammalian dive reflex.

items.title: Trump says new Air Force One will be sent away for upgrades amid security concerns
items.summary: President Trump said the Air Force One plane that was donated by Qatar will be sent away for upgrades amid swirling concerns about its security features.

items.title: Morning news brief
items.summary: The U.S. carries out a wave of strikes against Iran, as fighting between the two escalates, Congress faces long to-do list as August recess approaches, Andy Burnham becomes U.K.'s 7th prime minister.

items.title: 3 women's world records set at the annual Lumberjack World Championships in Wisconsin
items.summary: Three new women's world records have been set at the Lumberjack World Championships in Hayward, Wisconsin

items.title: 5 family members found dead after flash flood in a scenic Utah canyon
items.summary: Five members of a Utah family have died after a flash flood swept through canyons in southern Utah where they were on a day outing

items.title: Trump celebrates the World Cup as a US victory as he presents trophy to the winner, Spain
items.summary: President Donald Trump has presented the World Cup trophy to Spain, the winner of Sunday’s final against Argentina

items.title: Ferran Torres anota el gol que vale un Mundial para España
items.summary: Con un hombre más en el campo, el futbolista del FC Barcelona apareció dentro del área y sacó un disparo imparable para el "Dibu" Martínez.

items.title: US strikes Iran over troop deaths as Israel warns Tehran's attacks are coming close
items.summary: The United States has launched more airstrikes at Iran, targeting the Revolutionary Guard in response to the killing of U.S. troops

items.title: Justice Department activates untested court for 'alien terrorist' deportations
items.summary: The Justice Department has filed a first-ever petition to a secretive and dormant court created 30 years ago to weigh government requests to deport so-called “alien terrorists” from the United States

items.title: Andy Biggs and David Schweikert offer competing visions for Republican Party in Arizona primary
items.summary: Rep. Andy Biggs and Rep. David Schweikert are clashing in the Republican primary for Arizona governor

items.title: Taylor Farms recalls lettuce shipped to 27 states over cyclospora risk
items.summary: Taylor Farms has expanded a voluntary recall of its iceberg lettuce products sourced from central Mexico because of a potential link to the multistate cyclospora outbreak that has sickened people across the U.S. The California-based company said produc...

items.title: Texas Hill Country floods test new warning systems after last year’s deadly disaster
items.summary: Officials in Texas have implemented major changes to improve flood warnings after last year's deadly floods in Hill Country

items.title: License revoked for boarding school where Paris Hilton says she was abused as teen
items.summary: Utah has revoked another license for the boarding school where Paris Hilton said she was abused as a teenager

items.title: US cancels automatic protections for imperiled animals as critics warn of extinctions
items.summary: The U.S. Interior Department has canceled a rule meant to protect plants and animals that are determined to be threatened with extinction

items.title: Paul Pelosi charged with misdemeanor hit-and-run over collision with parked car
items.summary: Paul Pelosi has been charged with a misdemeanor hit-and-run after a collision earlier this month in California

items.title: Appeals court says 28-year sentence is too lenient for militant convicted in attack
items.summary: A federal appeals court has concluded that a 28-year prison sentence is too lenient for a Libyan militant who was convicted of terrorism-related charges in the 2012 attacks on U.S. compounds in Benghazi, Libya, that killed Ambassador Chris Stevens and ...

items.title: Federal appeals court rules that New Jersey's assault weapons ban is unconstitutional
items.summary: A federal appeals court ruled Friday that New Jersey’s bans on assault firearms and magazines that can hold more than 10 rounds are unconstitutional

items.title: A body is recovered after San Francisco boat tragedy but 2 remain missing
items.summary: Police have recovered the body of a woman who was one of three people missing after a boat sank this week in San Francisco Bay

items.title: Lawmakers demand answers after 'bombshell' report of ICE officer shooting in Maine
items.summary: Democratic members of Congress are demanding answers about Homeland Security’s vetting and training of immigration enforcement officers

items.title: Outrage after ICE agents tackle man at Las Vegas airport
items.summary: Video footage of ICE agents tackling a man at Las Vegas Airport has sparked public outrage and criticism from officials

items.title: Fleas are every pet parent’s worst nightmare – here’s how to get rid of them
items.summary: Vets say getting rid of fleas involves three steps: diagnosis, treatment and prevention, and cleaning. Here’s how to complete each one and products to use.

items.title: Everyone is talking about exosomes in skin care — here’s why
items.summary: Think of exosomes as microscopic delivery trucks that help repair skin tissue, increase collagen and more. I spoke to dermatologists about what exosomes do and how to safely use them.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
